steps.title: Identify the appositive and what follows
steps.content: The blank contains the appositive phrase carbon-13 (13C), which renames a rare carbon isotope, and it is immediately followed by due to the higher 13C levels.

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule
steps.content:
When an appositive is followed by additional text, it needs a boundary comma after it, and the appositive itself must stay as a single unit.

Option D achieves this: carbon-13 (13C),. Options A and C wrongly place a comma inside the appositive between the term and its abbreviation; Option B lacks the closing comma.

This matches choice D — D is correct.
